reviewNotes: Source-level review complete for the obligatory-frame cluster. The Gettysburg Address and Second Inaugural provide documented primary-source grounding for Lincoln's construction of the Civil War as a test to be endured rather than a victory to be exploited. Both sources are confirmed in the PPW bibliography and american-civil-war-union source-pack. Advancing to source-review status; not yet ready for promoted-finding because passage-level anchoring and inter-annotator reliability checks remain outstanding (see promotionBlockers).

scoreImpact.rationale: If passage-level evidence confirms the obligatory-frame cluster at scale, this claim would support the anti-sacrificial-restraint mechanism score for american-civil-war-union and reinforce the bounded-sacrifice reading that distinguishes the Union case from escalatory comparators.

limitations: Passage-level anchoring not yet completed; claim rests on document-level corpus inventory, not imported passage records
Cross-register frequency of the obligatory frame is cited anecdotally but not systematically measured
Obligatory-frame definition may overlap with sacrifice cluster; conceptual boundary requires evidence-review-level clarification
Review is source-only; evidence-review and argument-review gates have not yet been cleared
promotionBlockers: Passage records must be imported (PPW #143) before advancing to promoted-finding
